rationale: The cited paper examines conspicuous brand usage (including Facebook stimuli), impression-management inferences, and resulting attitude effects, while the citing paper’s core mechanism is virtual identity signaling from public social-media product posts. This strong construct/context overlap (conspicuous/public signaling via posts) suggests the citation could be a substantive theory/mechanism source rather than a generic mention. The pair is also directly matched by DOI (10.1509/jmr.11.0342).
